tweets.text:
$SPX Here's your alpha for next week:

Yes Window Dress EOQ rebalancing is next Tuesday. Anticipate some volatility eod for notable names. With that said...

We are entering a very seasonally strong July, where the S&P500 has closed July green 13 of the last 14 years.

Also next week, Friday is a stock holiday to observe Independence Day.

Here are how the last 15 July 4th holiday days performed (the last trading day before July 4th):

2025: +.83%
2024: +.51%
2023: +.12%
2022: +1.06%
2021: +.75%
2020: +.45%
2019: +.77%
2018: -.49%
2017: +.23%
2016: +.19%
2015: -.03%
2014: +.55%
2013:  +.08%
2012: +.62%
2011: +1.44%

For an average gain of +.47%. Trade accordingly on Thursday!

Do not short 'Murica!

-Heis

tweets.text:
All fair points. 

 I have no clue how high this cycle ultimately goes, but I do think BTC will likely continue to see diminishing returns from here. It’ll be really interesting to watch whether SOL or HYPE can pull off what ETH did in past cycles with meaningful new ATHs.

Totally agree SOL is sitting in a solid value area based on last cycles move and with a long enough time horizon, I think 12-18 months. 

That said, I’m still cautious for more near-term downside since I'm not sure BTC has reached it's 4 year cycle low yet. I think that comes in Oct/Nov of this year which makes me think SOL is still in the June–Oct ‘22 phase of the cycle rather than the Dec ‘22–May ‘23.
